lenses.guidance: vv 1-9's twelve-stones memorial uses repetition — taken from the riverbed (v 8), set up at Gilgal (v 20) — to make the crossing instrument permanent. The structural form is monument: the river will resume, the stones will not.

vv 6-7 and 21-24's catechetical pattern — 'when your children ask, what do these stones mean?' — frames covenant memory as parental responsibility. To respond today is to keep a household's stories tied to God's action.

v 24's 'so that all the peoples of the earth might know the hand of the LORD is mighty' echoes Ex 14:18 (Egyptians know) and threads to Hab 2:14 (the earth filled with the knowledge of the LORD). A canonical witness-arc.
